index.name: Nifty Healthcare Index
index.description: Nifty Healthcare tracks 20 companies spanning pharma (Sun Pharma 24.6%, Dr Reddy's 13.5%, Cipla 11.2%), APIs (Divi's Labs 8.9%), and hospital operators (Apollo 8.5%, Max Healthcare 6.8%). Defensive sector with low correlation to economic cycles — chronic disease burden (diabetes, hypertension, cancer) creates mandatory consumption. Dual growth engines: (1) Global generics export — India supplies 20% of world's generic medicines, 60% of vaccines to 180+ countries. Structural advantage: 60-70% cost discount vs US/Europe due to labor arbitrage and scale manufacturing. (2) Domestic branded generics — rising incomes, growing insurance coverage (Ayushman Bharat + private health insurance) driving 15-20% annual growth. Currency tailwind: pharma exports denominated in USD/EUR; when rupee weakens 10%, export profitability rises proportionally. Regulatory risk: US FDA denials create profit volatility, biosimilars margins compress from 50% to 20% as complexity increases. Long-term secular growth: India's per-capita healthcare spending ₹700 vs US ₹6,000 suggests 8-10 year runway for 20% annual growth.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceutical
holdings.description: India's largest pharma company and top-5 globally in specialty generics. Taro Pharma (USA), Ilumya/Cequa specialty brands, Winlevi dermatology — building US specialty beyond generics. Strong R&D pipeline.

holdings.name: Dr Reddy's Laboratories
holdings.description: Hyderabad-based generic pharma giant. US generics, branded formulations in India and Russia, API manufacturing. Revlimid (lenalidomide) para-IV authorized generic provides multi-year earnings boost.

holdings.name: Cipla Ltd
holdings.description: Piramal Group's pharma division — India's third largest. Strong US inhalation business (Advair generics pipeline). Africa branded generics. Cipla brand dominates respiratory and HIV in India.

holdings.name: Divi's Laboratories
holdings.description: World's largest manufacturer of APIs for regulated markets. Key supplier to top 10 global pharma. Carotenoids business and nutraceuticals. Asset-light model with industry-leading EBITDA margins of 35%+.

holdings.name: Apollo Hospitals
holdings.description: India's largest private hospital network with 70+ hospitals. Apollo HealthCo digital platform and Apollo Pharmacy chain. Chronic disease management, oncology, and organ transplants as key specialities.

holdings.name: Max Healthcare
holdings.description: North India's premium hospital chain with 17 hospitals (3,400+ beds). Oncology Centre of Excellence. Max Smart Super Speciality Hospital, Saket is flagship. IPO proceeds funding brownfield expansion.

holdings.name: Fortis Healthcare
holdings.description: Multi-specialty hospital chain across India and overseas. IHH Healthcare (Malaysia) is majority owner providing capital. SRL Diagnostics integration strengthens pathology vertical.

holdings.name: Narayana Hrudalaya
holdings.description: Affordable cardiac care at scale. Rabindranath Tagore International Institute of Cardiac Sciences. Health City Cayman Islands offshore medical tourism. ₹5,000 open-heart surgery model.

holdings.name: Torrent Pharma
holdings.description: Gujarat-based pharma with strong branded domestic portfolio. Germany (Heumann Pharma) and US operations. Cardiovascular, CNS, and gastro therapies dominate India prescriptions.

holdings.name: Lupin Ltd
holdings.description: Complex generics specialist: inhalation (Spiriva generic), injectables, biosimilars (Etanercept). US and Japan market exposure. Domestic branded Lupin franchise in TB, cardiovascular, diabetology.

index.name: Nifty Healthcare Index
index.description: Nippon India ETF Nifty Healthcare tracks 20 companies across the full healthcare value chain  hospitals (Apollo, Fortis, Max, Narayana), diagnostics (Metropolis, Dr. Lal Path, Thyrocare), pharmaceutical manufacturers (Sun Pharma, Cipla, Dr. Reddy's), and medical devices. India's healthcare sector is underpenetrated: healthcare spending at 3.2% of GDP vs 10-17% in developed countries. India's aging population (300M people above 60 by 2050), rising lifestyle diseases (diabetes, hypertension, obesity), increasing health insurance penetration (from 16% to 40%+ target) and medical tourism ($9B market growing 22% p.a.) are structural drivers. Hospitals-to-population ratio needs to triple by 2030  creating massive capex opportunity. India is also the world's pharmacy (produces 20% of global generic medicines by volume), supplying WHO with 50%+ of vaccines required. Healthcare ETF provides defensively growing exposure  hospital occupancies and prescription drug demand are non-cyclical.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries
holdings.description: India's largest pharma company and world's 5th largest generics maker. US specialty pharma business (Ilumya, Winlevi, Cequa) growing 20%+ p.a. India branded generics franchise (30,000+ MRs, No.1 in 14 therapeutic categories). Taro Pharma (Canada/US subsidiary) fully consolidated.

holdings.name: Apollo Hospitals Enterprise
holdings.description: India's largest private hospital chain  73+ hospitals, 10,000+ beds. Apollo HealthCo (digital health + pharmacy) growing 30%+ p.a. Apollo 24|7 telemedicine crossed 40M consultations. Fortis-like acquisitions still possible. Oncology, cardiac and neurology centres highly profitable.

holdings.name: Cipla
holdings.description: Known for affordable HIV, respiratory and cardiac generics. Inhalation (Advair biosimilar) targeting USD 1B+ US opportunity. South Africa is Cipla's largest export market. Post Blackstone investment, corporate governance and capital allocation improving significantly.

holdings.name: Dr. Lal PathLabs
holdings.description: India's largest diagnostics chain  5,000+ collection points, 250+ labs, 10M+ tests/year. Highly cash-generative business model (asset-light labs + phlebotomist fleet). Post-COVID health-consciousness driving sustained volumes. Expanding into tier-2/tier-3 markets via hub-and-spoke model.

holdings.name: Fortis Healthcare
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest listed hospital chain  36 hospitals, 4,900+ beds. Fortis Escorts Heart Institute and Fortis Memorial Research Institute are flagship centres. IHH Healthcare (Malaysia) holds 65%  global healthcare management expertise. Medical tourism hub for cardiac and orthopaedic procedures.

holdings.name: Max Healthcare
holdings.description: Fastest-growing large hospital chain  4,700+ beds across North India. Max Super Speciality Hospitals in Delhi/NCR dominate the affluent urban patient segment. Oncology, cardiology and transplant programmes attracting international patients. KKR backing (32% stake) brings global healthcare operations expertise.

name: Aditya Birla Sun Life MF-Aditya Birla Sun Life Nifty Healthcare ETF

index.name: Nifty Healthcare TRI
index.description: Aditya Birla Sun Life MF-Aditya Birla Sun Life Nifty Healthcare ETF tracks the Nifty Healthcare TRI index.

holdings.name: Sun Pharmaceuticals
holdings.description: India's largest pharma company and top 5 globally in specialty. Dermatology (Winlevi, Cequa), oncology and ophthalmics drive US margins of 30%+. 40 manufacturing facilities globally. Ilumya (US) and Levulan ramp the specialty pipeline.

holdings.name: Apollo Hospitals Enterprise
holdings.description: India's largest private hospital chain. 70+ hospitals, 10,000+ beds. Apollo 24|7 telemedicine platform: 50M+ users. Medical tourism leader (25%+ market share in international patients to India). Pharmacy chain Apollo Pharmacy — 6,000+ outlets.

holdings.name: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories
holdings.description: Among India's top 3 pharma exporters. Speciality generics in US, biosimilars in Europe and emerging markets. Revlimid (lenalidomide) AG deal and filgrastim biosimilar generating multi-year annuity cash flows.

holdings.name: Cipla
holdings.description: Pioneer of affordable branded generics. Inhalation therapy (Advair equivalents) for US, South Africa, India. Cipla Quality Chemical Industries (Africa) producing ARV drugs for PEPFAR. Strong ANDA pipeline with 30+ pending approvals.

holdings.name: Divi's Laboratories
holdings.description: India's dominant API (Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient) manufacturer. Contract manufacturing for top 10 global pharma cos (confidential). Divi's DSP division for complex chemistry. China+1 strategy accelerates order migration to Indian CDMOs.

holdings.name: Max Healthcare
holdings.description: North India's premium hospital operator (19 hospitals, 5,000+ beds). Revenue per bed growing due to complex surgeries, oncology and international patients. Saket and Patparganj campuses are tertiary care landmarks in Delhi NCR.
